excerpt: Position Objective: The Market Logistics Coordinator supports the Market Logistics Operations and the local operations team by handling administrative, communication and scheduling duties. Position Responsibilities: Coordinates with local branches to ensure all contracts are opened and closed timely to support efficient dispatch operations; may also close work orders and ensures all pertinent information is documented accurately and timely. Performs regular reviews of driver logs and schedules based on the direction of the Dispatcher. Acts as primary communication link between branches and the Market Logistics Operation Center. Reviews orders for correct contact and delivery instructions. Schedules third party haulers as needed at the direction of the Dispatcher. Keeps branches informed of next run out to facilitate proper staging of equipment. Monitors future orders to identify gaps in fleet availability. Assists dispatcher in prioritizing pickups based on need and time.

Position Objective: Manages Drivers across a market to ensure prompt receipt and delivery of equipment from/to customers and other Sunbelt locations in accordance with company and DOT standards. Position Responsibilities: Orchestrates the timely and accurate dispatch of rental equipment and tools to customers across assigned market or geography. Orchestrates the transfer of equipment and consumables across the defined market as needed. Creates and monitors drivers' schedules. Reviews daily and weekly driver logs in accordance with company and DOT standards. Ensures drivers are performing daily truck inspections in accordance with company and DOT standards. Works closely with store locations to ensure customer's equipment needs are met. Provides adequate feedback to the Counter and Service Departments regarding the timely delivery and receipt of equipment. Works with Dispatch Coordinators and branch locations to ensure driver on yard time is minimal.

By using visual timelines, real-time GPS, auto-routing, and a mobile driver app, Logistics Solution simplifies rental transportation operations, enhances communication between drivers and dispatchers – ensuring on time pick-ups and deliveries. Users have seen a 3-day reduction in pickup time, along with gains in fuel efficiency, team productivity, outside hauling cost reduction, and improved load utilization – all of which enhance rental transportation efficiency.

Dispatch management in equipment rental covers the coordination of all physical movements of rental equipment: delivery to customers, collection at the end of rental, inter-depot transfers and collection of items for maintenance. Effective dispatch management prevents late deliveries, reduces wasted driver trips, ensures equipment is returned before the next booking needs it, and creates an audit trail for every movement in the fleet. Rental software flags these conflicts automatically — giving dispatch managers the information to intervene early, source alternative equipment or contact the affected customer.

Common causes include: unclear ETAs and changing delivery windows, manual route planning that doesn’t adapt quickly, limited visibility into driver workloads and load status, and 'one-off' dispatch decisions that can’t scale across many moves. Dispatchers can drag and drop loads to the appropriate driver, automatically calculate route durations, and adjust schedules as needed. The Scheduler Dashboard also supports predispatching, allowing loads to be assigned in advance and dispatched in groups. For teams running high-volume moves, predispatching can reduce last-minute decisions and create a more predictable dispatch rhythm.

Poor dispatch leads to: Missed or late deliveries, Emergency re-runs and extra fuel, Overtime for drivers and yard staff, Crews standing idle on jobsites, Equipment sitting unused when it should be earning, Lost customer trust that doesn’t always get reported. Top rental companies make sure drivers leave with: Clear delivery and collection details, Accurate equipment lists, Correct jobsite locations, Notes about access, timing, or site restrictions. Dispatch Must Account for Returns, Not Just Deliveries.

The Yard Associate is responsible for the stationing and retrieving equipment on the location's property. The role maintains a written maintenance schedule and may service equipment as necessary to ensure equipment meets all set specifications. The Yard Associate also inspects equipment prior to and after rental to document any lost and/or damaged equipment.
